channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-7352] conf-sanity test_78: no space left on device
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/fe434a72-7d5b-11e5-bca9-5254006e85c2.

The sub-test test_78 failed with the following error:

1048576 bytes (1.0 MB) copied, 0.140863 s, 7.4 MB/s
dd: opening `/mnt/lustre/d78.conf-sanity/f78.conf-sanity-99': No space left on device
 conf-sanity test_78: @@@@@@ FAIL: (4) create /mnt/lustre/d78.conf-sanity/f78.conf-sanity-99 failed

Please provide additional information about the failure here.

Info required for matching: conf-sanity 78
Info required for matching: conf-sanity 79
item.summary: conf-sanity test_78: no space left on device

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
Oleg Drokin (oleg.drokin@intel.com) merged in patch http://review.whamcloud.com/20558/
Subject: LU-7352 tests: conf-sanity 78 don't check missing files
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 46319ab0903ccd1a28a3011eef995e7f5cfe334e

Andreas Dilger (andreas.dilger@intel.com) uploaded a new patch: http://review.whamcloud.com/20558
Subject: LU-7352 tests: conf-sanity 78 don't check missing files
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: d6a01df3686ce965e2ff268a8f667f926f9bd1e8

Andreas Dilger (andreas.dilger@intel.com) merged in patch http://review.whamcloud.com/20166/
Subject: LU-7352 tests: don't fail conf-sanity test_78 on ENOSPC
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: b666d02e13e0890a244910221c5833d2af39aec9

Hmm..one point is MDS is trying to balance OST objects allocation between different
OST, trying to allocate new objects on those with more free blocks.

Whatever way, users will still fail to create empty files, when all OST
are running out of this check..

Let's think this problem more.

Now lod_qos_dev_is_full() is always checking using bavail which will except
Granting space, but MDS pre-create will only try to create empty objects on OST,
which dose not eat any free blocks here.

So if we skip granted space here and just use bfree blocks to check if we can
go on, that should be fine, considering when clients connect to OST, it should
have tried it best to get some grant space or just fall back to sync/direct write.

Another thing that I notcied is now it has following comments:
/* the minimum of 0.1% used blocks and 1GB bytes. */
I think this still not perfect, think carefull, with a big OST, we might lost 1GB space,
and I am wondering there exist a case, Users just want empty or small files, But it can
not go on because of this check.

The actual problem here is that there is only a single OST in the filesystem, and it is very close to running out of space, so when the MDT is trying to precreate objects it is seeing that bavail = 0 and skipping that OST, which is the only one. It isn't taking into account that the client may have reserved space on the OST that it wants to write to a new file. That said, if the MDT checked bfree it may continue to create files on that OST even though the client does not have any grant there.

I thought many years ago that the client should be able to send a bitmap of "preferred" OSTs and/or "forbidden" OSTs during create RPCs to the MDS. This would allow the client to select OSTs that are closer to itself by network topology, skip OSTs that it does not have a connection to, or that it doesn't have any grant for.

Looking at the client debug log from a recent failure it appears that the ENOSPC is coming from the MDS, possibly if there are no precreated objects on the OSTs yet since the filesystem was just formatted?
https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/sub_tests/eadbd53e-17f8-11e6-b5f1-5254006e85c2

(mdc_locks.c:536:mdc_finish_enqueue()) Process entered
(mdc_locks.c:590:mdc_finish_enqueue()) @@@ op: 3 disposition: 17, status: -28  req@ffff88004e7ca400 x1534075851521040/t0(0) o101->lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff88005ac84800@10.9.4.123@tcp:12/10 lens 888/544 e 0 to 0 dl 1463009294 ref 1 fl Complete:R/0/0 rc 301/301
(mdc_locks.c:719:mdc_finish_enqueue()) Process leaving (rc=0 : 0 : 0)
(mdc_locks.c:980:mdc_finish_intent_lock()) D_IT dentry f78.conf-sanity-97 intent: open|creat status -28 disp 17 rc 0

The MDS log seems to agree with that to some extent, but it is getting 0 available blocks back from the OST, so it is generating the ENOSPC locally:

(osp_precreate.c:129:osp_statfs_interpret()) Process entered
(osp_precreate.c:969:osp_pre_update_status()) lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000: status: 36034 blocks, 9261 free, 26 used, 0 avail -> -28: rc = 0
(osp_precreate.c:972:osp_pre_update_status()) non-committed changes: 0, in progress: 0
(osp_precreate.c:151:osp_statfs_interpret()) updated statfs ffff88004f038800
(osp_precreate.c:153:osp_statfs_interpret()) Process leaving (rc=0 : 0 : 0)

The OST was formatted with 45000 blocks = 175MB for the test:

   Permanent disk data:
Target:     lustre:OST0000
Index:      0
Lustre FS:  lustre
Mount type: ldiskfs
Flags:      0x62
              (OST first_time update )
Persistent mount opts: ,errors=remount-ro
Parameters: mgsnode=10.9.4.123@tcp sys.timeout=20

device size = 12472MB
formatting backing filesystem ldiskfs on /dev/lvm-Role_OSS/P1
	target name   lustre:OST0000
	4k blocks     45000
	options        -I 256 -q -O extents,uninit_bg,dir_nlink,quota,huge_file,flex_bg -G 256 -E lazy_itable_init,resize="4290772992",lazy_journal_init -F
mkfs_cmd = mke2fs -j -b 4096 -L lustre:OST0000  -I 256 -q -O extents,uninit_bg,dir_nlink,quota,huge_file,flex_bg -G 256 -E lazy_itable_init,resize="4290772992",lazy_journal_init -F /dev/lvm-Role_OSS/P1 45000

but there are 7 OSTs ~= 1225MB so it is strange that there isn't enough space available on this one OST. Hopefully the extra debugging patch will show what is happening.

Andreas Dilger (andreas.dilger@intel.com) uploaded a new patch: http://review.whamcloud.com/20166
Subject: LU-7352 tests: add debugging to conf-sanity test_78
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 46e82d0c3f2d94b5e579ca291df3137ef38c6612

Another failure on master:
https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/834acc42-090f-11e6-b5f1-5254006e85c2

The same failure still occurred on master branch:
https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/94f9085c-fd3c-11e5-8750-5254006e85c2

More failures on master:
https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/fe434a72-7d5b-11e5-bca9-5254006e85c2
2015-12-15 02:54:42 - https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/04050e8a-a2fc-11e5-9b3d-5254006e85c2
2015-12-30 07:03:26 - https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/0df22316-aef4-11e5-aa1f-5254006e85c2
This may relate to LU-7309.
